cases.title: P-101 High Vibration and Bearing Temperature Event
cases.problem_statement: P-101 developed increasing vibration, elevated bearing temperature and abnormal mechanical noise while lubrication evidence was missing.
cases.summary: Available evidence indicates that missed or degraded lubrication caused increased bearing friction, elevated temperature and mechanical vibration.

timeline.title: Lubrication evidence unavailable
timeline.description: The scheduled lubrication activity could not be verified from the available maintenance records.

timeline.title: Bearing temperature increased
timeline.description: The latest bearing temperature crossed its warning threshold and showed an increasing trend.

timeline.title: High vibration and abnormal noise confirmed
timeline.description: Inspection confirmed elevated vibration and abnormal mechanical noise near the bearing housing.

timeline.title: Corrective work order opened
timeline.description: A high-priority work order was created for lubrication, alignment and bearing inspection.

causal_chain.title: Lubrication activity not verified
causal_chain.description: The required lubrication task had no reliable completion evidence.

causal_chain.title: Bearing friction increased
causal_chain.description: Insufficient or degraded lubrication increased friction inside the bearing assembly.

causal_chain.title: Bearing temperature increased
causal_chain.description: Increasing friction produced abnormal thermal behaviour around the bearing.

causal_chain.title: Vibration and noise increased
causal_chain.description: Bearing degradation and possible misalignment caused vibration and abnormal mechanical noise.

root_causes.title: Missed or degraded bearing lubrication
root_causes.reasoning: The lubrication evidence gap occurred before the temperature and vibration escalation. These symptoms are consistent with increasing bearing friction.
counter_evidence: No lubricant laboratory analysis is currently available.

root_causes.title: Bearing wear or shaft misalignment
root_causes.reasoning: High vibration and abnormal noise near the bearing housing indicate possible bearing wear or alignment deterioration.
counter_evidence: Alignment measurements have not yet been recorded.

root_causes.title: Hydraulic instability or cavitation
root_causes.reasoning: Hydraulic instability may produce vibration and noise, but the available evidence more strongly supports a bearing-related problem.
counter_evidence: No confirmed suction-pressure anomaly is currently available.
The bearing-temperature increase is more consistent with mechanical friction.

corrective_actions.title: Inspect and restore bearing lubrication
corrective_actions.description: Safely isolate the pump, inspect lubricant condition and quantity, and restore lubrication according to the approved procedure.

corrective_actions.title: Perform vibration and alignment assessment
corrective_actions.description: Measure vibration spectrum, shaft alignment and bearing clearances before returning the asset to normal service.
corrective_actions.verification_metric: Vibration and alignment values remain within approved limits

corrective_actions.title: Add lubrication evidence control
corrective_actions.description: Require timestamped lubrication records and responsible-person confirmation before closing future work orders.
corrective_actions.verification_metric: Future lubrication work orders contain complete evidence

evidence.excerpt: Lubrication completion evidence for P-101 was not available during the compliance review.
The latest bearing-temperature condition was classified as warning with an increasing trend.
Inspection identified high vibration and abnormal mechanical noise near the bearing housing.
Inspect lubrication condition, shaft alignment and bearing health before returning the pump to service.

cases.recommendation_summary: Treat lubrication degradation as the primary cause until lubricant condition, alignment measurements and vibration-spectrum analysis confirm or reject the hypothesis.
